name: Tuna Noodle Casserole
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Plain or seasoned bread crumbs
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat the oven to 375°F.
2. Place the rack in the center position and grease a medium-sized baking dish, or two smaller dishes, with non-stick vegetable cooking spray.
3. Cook the noodles according to package directions, but reduce cooking time by 2 minutes. Drain the noodles and stir them into the peas, soup, and milk mixture.
4. Mash the tuna with a fork to break it up and add it to the noodle mixture. Stir gently and pour into the prepared baking dish.
5. Sprinkle bread crumbs over the top of the casserole and bake for 35 minutes, until hot.
Variations:
Add salt (taste first, as soup may be salted enough); freshly grated black pepper; white pepper; snipped fresh herbs such as tarragon, or dried herbs. If you prefer fewer bread crumbs, toss 1/2 to 3/4 cup with 1 tablespoon melted butter or margarine for a light sprinkling.
